sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 4-year-old's mother, presented with a complaint of right knee pain after a skiing accident.
She fell while coming off a lift and caught her ski, causing her knee to pop.
She was unable to bear weight and was taken down by the ski patrol.
The pain is located on the outside of the knee and feels like it won't hold her weight.
She has had difficulty bending and straightening the knee since the injury.
She has not experienced numbness or tingling in her toes.
Past medical history: The patient is otherwise healthy, with no history of high blood pressure, diabetes, or other medical conditions.
She has twins at home and her husband is often away for work, leaving her to care for the children.
Review of systems: The patient denies any additional symptoms in other organs.
Current medications: The patient is taking ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
Objective:
Vital signs: [NOT MENTIONED]
Physical exam: Examination of the right knee revealed edema and an effusion.
Palpation of the right lateral knee caused pain.
There was decreased flexion and extension, and a positive Lachman sign.
Palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulses were present, and no leg edema was found in the ankle.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient likely has a ruptured anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) in her right knee.
Problem list: Right knee pain; ACL injury; knee effusion.
Plan:
I prescribed ibuprofen 600-800mg every 8 hours as needed for pain.
I ordered a knee MRI to confirm the suspected ACL injury.
I also recommended a knee brace to provide additional support.
I will follow up with the patient to discuss the results of the MRI and determine the next steps for treatment.
Follow-up: The patient will be seen again soon to discuss the results of the MRI and plan for further treatment.
